title: Semi-Automatic Open-Mouth Bagging Machine
content:
Semi-Automatic Open-Mouth Bagging Machine

A semi-automatic open-mouth bagging machine combines mechanical automation with a controlled level of manual assistance. The main frame is built from durable metal structures that remain stable under long hours of heavy-duty operation. Inside the machine, sensors capture accurate weight and position data, pneumatic components drive each motion with smooth responsiveness, and the electrical control system coordinates the full sequence to keep the process steady and reliable.

How to choose between a single-station and a double-station semi-automatic bagging machine?

A single-station model works well for factories producing up to 300–400 bags per hour or facilities with limited floor space. Medium-scale operations that expect continuous output often choose a double-station design, as the alternating workflow helps reduce idle time and increase overall efficiency.

What types of bags can be used with a semi-automatic open-mouth bagging machine?

The semi-automatic open-mouth bagging machine is compatible with paper bags, woven bags, and PE bags. It handles common flat-top bags and M bags, though final confirmation is needed to ensure bag size, weight, and the bag clamp design are fully aligned.

Can a semi-automatic bagging machine be upgraded to a fully automatic packaging line later?

A semi-automatic system can be integrated with automatic weighing, sealing, and palletizing modules. When the time comes to expand into a fully automatic line, the existing machine does not need to be replaced.

Is special treatment required for dusty production environments?

In dust-heavy workshops, it is recommended to add a dust-collection device and use stainless steel for material-contact components. This reduces dust leakage during filling and helps prevent corrosion on key machine surfaces.

title: Automatic Open-Mouth Bagging Machine
content:
Automatic Open-Mouth Bagging Machine

Automatic bagging systems vary widely in output, configuration, and compatibility with different materials and bag types. Choosing the right model depends on daily production volume, the diversity of products handled, and how much automation a plant wants to introduce. Our automated bagging systems combine reliable mechanics with precise control, supporting consistent, high-quality packaging as production volumes rise.

Which bagging machine is suitable for small businesses with only a few hundred bags per day?

For small-scale operations with modest output, lower-capacity automatic models offer a balanced choice. Options such as 3CM-5B (60–150 bags/hour) or DTB-W (200–300 bags/hour) deliver stable performance without requiring a large investment. These machines meet the needs of seasonal, multi-product, or limited-volume production while keeping maintenance simple.

What should medium-sized operations choose when producing several thousand bags per day?

Medium-scale factories benefit from models with moderate throughput, such as 3CM-P (600–800 bags/hour) or DTB-Z (500–700 bags/hour). These systems support continuous production with compatibility for paper, woven, or PE bags. They can be paired with heat sealing or sewing systems, making them flexible enough for common materials in food, feed, and chemical packaging. This capacity range provides a practical balance between efficiency and investment.

What type of bagging machine suits large-scale operations producing tens of thousands of bags per day?

High-capacity plants require high-speed, continuous-duty models. Options like 3CM-5U (1000 bags/hour) or the GXB series (800–1000 bags/hour) are built for intensive, around-the-clock production. These machines feature multiple bag magazines, reliable bag-feeding systems, and servo-driven arms for accurate bag placing. They minimize manual intervention and integrate smoothly with downstream sealing and palletizing lines, supporting large automatic packaging networks.

How to choose the right automatic bagging machine when handling multiple bag types and materials?

Different models offer their own compatibility strengths. The 3CM-P series supports paper, woven, and plastic bags within a 20–50 kg range, making it suitable for mixed material production. The GXB arm-type bagger provides even greater adaptability, especially when bag stiffness and material vary. Its servo-driven arm improves precision during bag pickup and placement. These machines can be paired with folding, sewing, or heat-sealing modules to ensure clean, consistent sealing quality across diverse products.

title: Open-Mouth Bagging Machines
content:
Bagging and Palletizing System

Detai specializes in engineering and manufacturing integrated bagging and palletizing systems, including open-mouth bagging machines, bag sealing machines, bag sewing machines, bagging scales, and automated bag palletizing systems.

Open-Mouth Bagging Machines
 Choosing the right open-mouth bagging machine depends on production scale, budget, and the level of automation required. Different operations face different challenges, so selecting the most suitable configuration can improve efficiency and keep investment under control. Our open-mouth bagging systems give manufacturers a reliable way to scale output, reduce manual work, and improve overall packaging efficiency.

Which type of bagging machine is suitable for a newly established bulk-material packaging plant with a limited budget?

For early-stage businesses, a semi-automatic model is often the best choice. It requires a smaller investment, occupies less floor space, and works well for multi-variety, small-batch, or seasonal production.

What equipment is recommended for medium-scale bulk-material operations that need higher throughput and reduced labor?

A fully automatic bagging machine is the ideal option. It helps cut labor requirements and delivers a good balance between speed and stability. This type of system suits common materials such as grain, feed, and chemicals.

What solutions are suitable for large-scale plants that run high-volume, continuous production?

Open-mouth bagging machine models such as the 3CM-5U (up to 1000 bags/hr) or the GXB arm-type bagging machine (800–1000 bags/hr) are well-suited for continuous, around-the-clock production. These systems integrate seamlessly with downstream sealing and palletizing equipment to support large-capacity operations.

What is the main difference between semi-automatic and fully automatic bagging equipment?

Semi-automatic systems require manual bag placement or assisted positioning. They offer a lower investment cost and suit small to medium output. Fully automatic machines handle bag pickup, opening, placing, and conveying without manual involvement, enabling higher efficiency and reduced labor use.

FBH Series

PP/PE Bag Heat Sealing Machine

Continuous heated-bar bag sealer for heavy-duty plastic open-mouth bags

The heat-sealing machines are designed for closing heavy-duty PE and PP bags, as well as paper–plastic and aluminum-laminated composite bags. The system uses a TEFLON (PTFE) sealing belt to prevent sticking during the sealing process and to maintain a clean, uniform seal. This configuration ensures stable sealing quality even with thicker multi-layer materials, making it suitable for demanding industrial applications where strength and reliability matter.

![PP/PE Bag Heat Sealing Machine](https://autopackingsystem-en.com/products/2-1-1-pp-pe-bag-heat-sealing-machine_04.webp)

FBH-32D PP/PE Bag Heat Sealing Machine
 Process Flow Diagram

Bag-top cleaning → Optional trimming knife to prepare and straighten the bag mouth → plastic-bag heat sealing (three heating-block groups + one cooling block; the PTFE sealing belt keeps the heating block from contacting the bag directly, preventing sticking and protecting the outer surface) → pressure rollers for sealing compression → plastic-bag cooling (two cooling-block groups) → knurling by pressure rollers

The woven bag heat sealing and stitching machine is designed for woven bags (including laminated woven bags) and supports heat sealing of the inner PE liner while applying a crepe-paper tape to the outer surface.  The woven bag heat sealing and stitching machine is designed for PE-lined woven bags and similar packaging materials. It uses a two-stage sealing process: the inner liner is heat-sealed first, followed by the application of a hot-melt adhesive tape on the outer layer. title: Heated-Bar Bag Sealing Machine
content:
Heated-Bar Bag Sealing Machine

A heated-bar bag sealing machine uses a solid metal heating block, generally aluminum or copper, to transfer heat through surface contact. This creates a broad, smooth seal line, usually between 5–20 mm, suitable for heavy-duty or multi-layer packaging materials. With precise temperature control within ±1°C, the system prevents overheating or incomplete fusion, ensuring clean and reliable sealing performance.

The bag sealing machine can seal materials from 0.5 mm up to 5 mm in thickness, including paper–plastic composite bags and aluminum-laminated woven bags. The heating block can also be customized in different shapes, such as curved or contoured designs, to accommodate special bag formats like gusseted bags or square-bottom bags. This flexibility makes the equipment suitable for demanding industrial environments that require strong, airtight, and visually consistent seals.

title: Impulse Bag Sealing Machine
content:
Impulse Bag Sealing Machine

The impulse bag sealer uses a fine metal heating wire (such as nichrome) that heats instantly when energized. Heat is transferred through a narrow line of contact, producing a thin, clean seal band. Because the heating wire has low resistance and minimal thermal inertia, it can rise from room temperature to around 200°C in just 1–3 seconds. This allows fast cycling and reduces the risk of overheating the film. It is ideal for sealing 0.1–0.5 mm films such as PE and PP bags, especially where appearance and sealing precision matter.

What types of bags and materials are suitable for impulse sealing?

Impulse sealing works well for thin-film bags between 0.1–0.5 mm, including single-layer PE and PP films or lightweight laminated films. The narrow sealing mark makes it suitable for products that require a clean finish, such as food packaging or lightweight chemical bags.

Which impulse sealer is recommended for small food packages (thin-film bags)?

Small food packages typically use PE or PP film bags and require fast sealing with a neat finish. The IMS impulse sealer or BD+7TG plastic film sealer is recommended for these applications.

Which sealing machine is suitable for high-capacity, continuous packaging lines?

For automated or continuous production, the QF pneumatic film sealing machine is recommended. Pneumatic clamping ensures consistent pressure, and the fast heat-wire response supports high-speed operation. It can also be paired with automatic bagging machines for thin-film bag applications in industries such as chemicals, feed, and grain.

What sealing machine should be used for vacuum packaging or products requiring high airtightness?

Products such as pharmaceuticals, food items, or electronic components that require airtight sealing benefit from the HL-Q vacuum sealing machine. The system evacuates the bag before sealing to reduce oxygen and moisture, making it suitable for vacuum bags, foil bags, and laminated films while extending product shelf life.

title: Bag Top Folding Device (Bag Infeed Device)
content:
Bag Top Folding Device (Bag Infeed Device)

The bag top folding device is an automated pre-sealing system developed by Detai to prepare the mouth of the bag before stitching. By folding the top edge of the bag into a neat, reinforced layer, it improves sealing strength and helps prevent issues such as leakage, loose stitching, or opening during transport. This bag infeed device is commonly installed at the front end of an automatic bag closing system to ensure packaging quality remains stable and consistent across the production line.

A production line often experiences weak bag sealing or occasional leakage. Which folding device should be selected?

To enhance sealing strength and improve overall bag presentation, a belt-type bag top folding device or a rubber-bar chain folding device is recommended. Both create a firm, uniform fold, making the stitched seam more secure.

If the packaging line handles large bags or requires an extended folding length, which model is most suitable?

The GP8600 extended belt-type folding device is ideal for large bag openings or applications requiring a longer folding stroke. Its extended structure provides greater control, producing stable, uniform folds even with oversized packaging. It is widely used in food, chemical, and feed industries.

How should the folding device be selected based on the bag material?

- Paper bags or woven bags with moderate thickness:
 Recommended models include the GP4900 belt-type folding device and the GP8600 extended belt-type folding device, both offering clean, consistent folding for these materials.

- Composite bags, laminated film bags, or high-speed production requirements:
 The GP6000 or GP1360 rubber-bar chain folding devices are better suited. Their folding mechanism maintains accuracy and stability even at high operating speeds or with bags that require a more controlled folding force.

title: Bag Sealing Machines
content:
Which types of bags are suitable for heated-bar sealing machines used in bulk material packaging?

Heated-bar sealers work well with materials ranging from 0.5–5 mm in thickness. They are compatible with paper–plastic composite bags, aluminum-lined woven bags, and multilayer laminated bags. The heating block can be customized in different shapes, such as curved or profiled designs, to match special bag styles like gusseted or square-bottom bags.

What is the difference between heated-bar sealing and heated-wire sealing?

Heated-bar sealing transfers heat through a broad contact surface. The temperature remains even, making it suitable for thick or multi-layer laminated bags. The seal is wide, flat, and consistent, and the block can be tailored for special-shaped bags. Heated-wire sealing uses a thin heating element and is ideal for film bags or lightweight packaging. The seal is narrow, but the sealing speed is faster.

Servo Gravity Feed Bagging Scale

Servo-controlled gravity feeding scale for dosing and weighing of granular and powder materials

The servo gravity feed bagging scale uses a servo-driven feeding system instead of the traditional dual-cylinder setup. This upgrade delivers faster response, steadier operation, and higher weighing accuracy while eliminating fluctuations caused by air-pressure instability.

The weighing structure adopts three high-precision load cells arranged at equal angles around the scale’s center of gravity. This layout prevents uneven force distribution which is a common issue in multi-sensor systems, and ensures consistent, high-accuracy measurement during every filling cycle.

This servo-controlled gravity feeding scale is widely used in fertilizer and chemical production. title: Bagging Scale
content:
Bagging Scale
 Selecting the right bagging scale is essential for achieving consistent weighing accuracy, stable material flow, and smooth integration with downstream packaging equipment. Detai provides a range of weighing solutions engineered for grain, feed, chemical, fertilizer, and other bulk-material industries, helping production lines maintain speed, precision, and clean operation.

What technical indicators matter most to grain processing plants when choosing a bagging scale?

Grain producers typically focus on weighing accuracy, packaging speed, dust-control design, and compatibility with existing conveyors. Our belt-feed bagging scales achieve ±0.2% accuracy, while gravity-feed systems can reach ±0.1%, ensuring reliable performance across different grain types.

What feeding method should be used for crops like corn or wheat with varying particle sizes?

Belt-feed bagging scales are suitable for medium and large particle sizes, providing steady flow without clogging. For high-volume free-flowing grain, gravity-feed ton-bag scales deliver stable throughput and smooth operation.

How can pet food manufacturers avoid cross-contamination when switching frequently between different recipes?

A modular quick-clean design is recommended. Systems equipped with automatic cleaning functions and removable pipelines help reduce residue and prevent formulation cross-mixing during product changeovers.

How do you prevent high-fat or oily pellets from sticking during packaging?

This can be solved by selecting non-stick conveyor belts, integrating an automatic oil-mist removal system, and using food-grade non-stick coatings inside the weighing chamber to maintain smooth material flow.

title: Automatic Bagging System for Swine Feed Pellet Packaging
content:
![Animal Feed Packaging](https://autopackingsystem-en.com/products/animal-feed-packaging_01.webp)

Automatic Bagging System for Swine Feed Pellet Packaging
 The animal feed sector covers a wide mix of pelletized products, and efficient handling of these materials plays an important role in maintaining smooth bagging, sealing, and palletizing operations across the production line. Swine feed in particular requires controlled dosing, consistent bag presentation, and reliable sealing to maintain feed quality throughout storage and distribution.

Project Requirements

This project involved a packaging line for swine feed pellets with detailed specifications for equipment size, control functions, weighing accuracy, and overall capacity. The upright conveyor and overturning conveyor had to be produced according to designated length, width, and height. The upright conveyor required a foot-pedal switch for start and stop control. The reject device had to use a pneumatic pusher. The compression conveyor needed a square-roller structure. The robotic unit had to operate with a single gripper. Line capacity had to remain within 600–800 bags per hour, and the checkweigher needed an accuracy of ±20 g. The customer also required compatibility with a Desuper GS-7C sewing head and an interface for an external inkjet coder.

Our Solution

Detai supplied a fully customized system based on these functional and dimensional requirements. The equipment package integrated precise weighing, stable rejection, and reliable sewing performance, ensuring consistent bag quality throughout the process. All motors were configured with SEW units to maintain stable operation and meet performance expectations. The tailored design ensured that every component aligned with the customer’s application needs while supporting accurate filling, dependable sealing, and efficient pellet handling.

title: Automatic Bagging System for PVC Resin Granule Packaging
content:
![Chemical Packaging](https://autopackingsystem-en.com/products/chemical-packaging_01.webp)

Automatic Bagging System for PVC Resin Granule Packaging
 The chemical packaging industry handles a broad variety of powders and granules, each with its own flow characteristics, dust behavior, and safety requirements. Packaging lines in this sector need stable filling performance, reliable sealing, and smooth handling of different bag types to keep production efficient and consistent.

- Product name (Material): PVC resin (polyvinyl chloride resin)

- Material form: Granular

- What we provided: Servo gravity dual-scale system + transition hopper + 3CM automatic open-mouth bagging machine + GKS sealing machine

A PVC resin manufacturer selected our automatic bagging system for packaging PVC (polyvinyl chloride) granules. The solution combined a servo-driven dual gravity scale, transition hopper, 3CM-PS automatic open-mouth bagging machine, and GKS-13 paper bag heat-sealing and stitching unit. This configuration ensured high-speed operation, compatibility with the required bag types, and full alignment with the customer’s performance and material standards.

Project Requirements

The packaging line needed to achieve an output of 800 bags per hour with a 25 kg filling weight. The specified bags were M-type multi-wall kraft paper sacks with a composite structure (three-in-one). The system also had to handle two empty bag sizes: 860 × 450 mm and 890 × 460 mm, and a filled bag dimension of 700 × 460 × 150 mm. All product-contact components were required to be stainless steel, while the remaining machine frame could be carbon steel. Fasteners such as screws and nuts needed to be stainless steel. Several electrical components had to be supplied in designated imported brands to ensure compatibility with the customer’s existing production environment.

Our Solution

A 3CM-PS automatic bagging machine with a capacity exceeding 800 bags per hour was configured for this project, fully matching the customer’s bag type and size specifications. All contact points were manufactured in 304 stainless steel, while the main frame used carbon steel for durability and structural integrity. This setup ensured hygienic handling of the material, stable filling performance, and reliable operation in line with the customer’s technical, structural, and integration requirements.

content:

A complete bag packaging line brings together every stage of filling, sealing, inspection, and palletizing into one coordinated system. From bagging and conveying to load preparation for shipment, each module works in sequence to create a smooth, continuous workflow. The layout below reflects the standard configuration used across grain, chemical, fertilizer, and feed production environments to meet their demands for stable output and precise handling.

Features

This gives the flexibility to configure the most suitable combination of bagging scales, bagging machines, sealing equipment, inspection systems, and palletizing units for different production environments.

- The internal structure of the packaging equipment is designed with dust collectors featuring air-pressure balancing and dust-recovery functions. It prevents dust from escaping during filling and protects the surrounding production environment from contamination.

- High-accuracy weighing is achieved through advanced anti-interference technology and precision weighing modules. Combined with optimized control algorithms and high-performance actuators, the system delivers stable operation and consistently high measuring accuracy.

- The packaging line incorporates weight inspection, metal detection, and rejection units to ensure that all outgoing products meet quality requirements.

- Key components come from internationally recognized brands such as Siemens PLC and touch panels, ABB robots, and Flintec weighing modules. These components provide dependable performance and intuitive operation. To support stable and safe production, the system also includes alarm functions and automatic fault-diagnosis features.

title: Automatic Bagging and Palletizing System for Compound Fertilizer Packaging
content:
Automatic Bagging and Palletizing System for Compound Fertilizer Packaging

![Fertilizer Packaging](https://autopackingsystem-en.com/products/fertilizer-packaging_01.webp)

The fertilizer packaging sector handles large volumes of granular and powder materials and requires equipment that can manage steady flow, fast bag changes, and reliable sealing. Production lines must remain stable even under high throughput, while also adapting to different bag sizes, stacking patterns, and product weights.

- Product name (Material): Compound fertilizer

- Bag material: Outer woven bag with inner PE liner

- Bag style: Single-fold open-mouth bag

- What we provided: Servo dual-scale system, automatic open-mouth bagging machine, high-speed bag sewing machine, ABB robotic palletizing system

A compound-fertilizer producer implemented our automatic bagging and palletizing solution to support fast, continuous packaging across multiple weight specifications. The project involved a complete line designed to handle conveying, dosing, bag placing, filling, sealing, palletizing, and automated line transitions.

Project Requirements

The packaging system needed to deliver a line capacity of at least 800 bags per hour while supporting three weight formats: 25 kg, 40 kg, and 50 kg. The packaging material consisted of woven bags with an inner PE liner, using a single-fold open-mouth structure. The line also required accurate rejection of non-conforming bags and quick switching between bag types to suit different production runs.

Our Solution

Detai supplied a full combination of servo weighing units, a fully automatic open-mouth bagging machine, a high-speed sewing system, and an ABB robotic palletizer. The system included vacuum-based bag detection, automatic bag rejection, and servo-driven one-touch adjustments to support smooth changeovers. These features ensured stable speed and consistent filling accuracy throughout the operation.

Key components in the line were designed for durability and hygienic handling. Critical contact areas were built from 304 stainless steel, while the conveyor belts used high-quality PVC materials suitable for fertilizer applications. The robotic gripper was made from high-strength aluminum alloy to maintain stability during high-speed stacking. All electrical and pneumatic components followed the customer’s designated brand requirements, including Siemens, Mettler Toledo, FESTO, and ABB, ensuring seamless integration with the customer’s existing systems.

This complete solution delivered high output, accurate filling, reliable sealing, and stable palletizing performance, meeting the customer’s expectations for efficiency, adaptability, and long-term operational reliability.

title: Dual-Station FIBC Bag Filling System for Food-Grade Glucose and Amino Acid Powder Packaging
content:
![Food Packaging](https://autopackingsystem-en.com/products/food-packaging_02.webp)

Dual-Station FIBC Bag Filling System for Food-Grade Glucose and Amino Acid Powder Packaging
 The food packaging industry spans a wide range of products and demands precise, efficient, and adaptable handling across every stage, including bagging, sealing, palletizing and depalletizing of different food items. A food producer selected our dual-station FIBC filling system for packaging glucose and valine powder. This project required strict compliance with food-grade hygiene standards, stable sealing performance, and reliable dust control.

![Food Packaging](https://autopackingsystem-en.com/products/food-packaging_01.webp)

Project Requirements

The project called for a system designed for food-grade operation. All contact surfaces required polished 304 stainless steel, and both soft connections and conveyor belts needed to meet food-grade material standards. The conveyor chain and rollers had to balance mechanical strength with corrosion resistance. The electrical system demanded an explosion-proof configuration, including certified motors and a capacitor-touch control panel. The production area also required a high-capacity dust-extraction setup with an airflow of at least 6000 m³/h, equipped with a recovery tray and explosion-relief outlet to maintain a clean and safe environment.

Our Solution

Detai supplied a system fully aligned with these requirements. All product-contact components were built from polished 304 stainless steel, and food-grade materials were used for soft connections and belts. BFM quick-release connectors were selected for safe and hygienic handling. The conveyor chain was nickel-plated carbon steel, while the rollers featured nickel-plated surfaces and chrome-plated internal shafts, ensuring both durability and corrosion resistance.

The electrical system followed a complete explosion-proof design, equipped with SEW explosion-proof motors and a capacitor-touch control interface. A high-efficiency dust-extraction system, also constructed in 304 stainless steel, delivered an airflow of ≥6000 m³/h and included both a recovery tray and an explosion-relief port. This configuration ensured a clean production area and full compliance with food-safety and explosion-protection standards.
